note: Source of truth for EQ moves by intent. Each entry resolves to a biquad. Used by pop/dsp/c (baked into eq_graph.c at build time — keep that table in sync when adding intents) and by pop/lib/master.mjs when surfacing knobs in node land.

sub.why: Strip subsonic. FM and small speakers can't reproduce it; it only wastes headroom.

rumble.why: Aggressive low cut for material with no real sub content — vocals, acoustic, narrow mixes.

warmth.why: Body without mud. Lifts chest of vocal / weight of synths without the 250 Hz cloud.

mud.why: Low-mid cloudiness when synths/sub-pad stack. Cut, don't boost.

chest.why: Vocal chest/body restore after a mud cut elsewhere in the chain.

boxy.why: The cardboard-tube honk on vocals and overdriven mid-range.  Narrow Q.

nasal.why: The pinched / nasal band on vocals + close-mic'd dialogue.

honk.why: Upper-mid bark on saws, leads, and bright synths.

harshness.why: Edginess on cymbals, top of guitars, and bright vocals.

presence.why: Forward, in-the-room. Lifts vocals/leads through a busy mix.

bite.why: Snap on transients — kick beater click, snare crack, picked guitar.

sibilance.why: S/T harshness on vocals. Narrow Q. Pair with presence boost lower down.

air.why: Open top — silk on vocals, sheen on cymbals, breath on synths.

sparkle.why: Glass-top, above where most mid-tier speakers reproduce. Mastering-only.

tilt-bright.why: Broad tilt for dark mixes. Use sparingly — mud removal usually beats top boost.

tilt-warm.why: Broad warm-tilt for thin material. Pairs with a small high-shelf cut, not boost.
